title: Runtime Posture Selective Handoff
goal: Make local execution posture and selective strong-reasoning handoff cheap enough to use in ordinary work.
Give weaker implementers a compact, posture-aware handoff contract that reduces planning drift and dishonest closeout.
non_goals: Do not hard-bind the workflow to one external vendor or model.
Do not widen this lane into a full runtime scheduler or a second planning authority.
Do not allow delegated workers to decide lane closure or larger-intent completion on their own.

intent.outcome: Ship the runtime posture loop that combines portable planning semantics, local execution posture, and compact strong-reasoning handoff guidance.
intent.constraints: Keep portable planning state runtime-agnostic, keep local posture repo-local, and make low-trust delegation visibly lower trust instead of silently equivalent to stronger execution.
intent.latitude: Shape the first implementation slice around the smallest compact runtime-resolution answer that materially changes day-to-day execution behavior.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the work would require a hidden planning store, vendor-specific orchestration lock-in, or broad rewrites across unrelated startup/compliance surfaces.

execution.next_step: Implement the compact runtime-resolution answer from the lane's suggested first slice, then layer the strong-reasoning handoff packet on top of that resolved posture.
execution.proof: Defaults/config/report surfaces expose a compact runtime-resolution answer that clearly distinguishes stay-local, stronger-reasoning, and manual external delegation paths, and focused CLI tests plus planning checks pass.

invariants: Portable execplans stay portable and do not embed machine-specific execution requirements.
Local posture and delegation-target truth stay in repo-local config or derived runtime payloads.
Delegated workers may receive bounded task packets but not lane-level closure authority.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make local execution posture and selective strong-reasoning handoff cheap enough to use in ordinary work, especially when weaker agents must implement bounded work under rate-limit pressure

required_continuation.activation trigger: continue until defaults/config/report surfaces can answer posture selection cheaply and strong handoff packets no longer require planner-written reconstruction

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: planned: a compact runtime-resolution answer and stronger handoff artifact can become the default planner-to-worker bridge instead of ad hoc prose.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: full automation or scheduling behavior, broader graceful-compliance cleanup, and any machine-first planning-store redesign outside the lane's direct runtime posture needs.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: recent weaker-agent failures show that trust classification, preflight guidance, and handoff compactness need to be treated as one operational loop rather than separate convenience features.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: none yet; this is a prepared plan.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: prove that the runtime-resolution answer changes ordinary startup behavior and that weaker agents can use the packet without widening scope or falsely closing lanes.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: implement issue #286's compact runtime-resolution answer, then issue #289's compact strong general-purpose reasoning handoff packet against that resolved posture.

intent_interpretation.literal request: prepare a full execplan for the first roadmap lane so a strong planner can bound implementation for weaker agents
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: turn the runtime-posture lane into an implementation-safe, machine-first plan that weaker agents can execute in bounded slices without reinterpreting the lane
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: plan the lane around the compact runtime-resolution answer first, with explicit handoff and closeout constraints that preserve planner authority over shaping and closure
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject implementations that add posture fields without changing the actionable answer to stay-local vs. stronger-reasoning vs. manual external delegation, or that let delegated execution masquerade as high-trust closure

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "delegation or runtime or summary or config"; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section startup --format json; uv run agentic-workspace config --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before changing planning storage contracts, repo bootstrap packaging, or broad startup/front-door doctrine unrelated to runtime posture selection
execution_bounds.stop before touching: packages/planning/ machine-first storage work, non-runtime roadmap prioritization, or local-only integration-area path creation

stop_conditions.stop when: the slice would need a broader planning-schema redesign or cannot produce a compact actionable answer from existing planning and config surfaces
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: portable execplan semantics need new canonical fields before posture resolution can be expressed honestly
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: the work starts solving generic startup discoverability or local-only integration storage instead of runtime posture and handoff
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: focused CLI tests cannot prove that runtime resolution and trust classification are queryable and actionable

context_budget.live working set: state.toml lane contract, local config posture fields, workspace CLI defaults/config/report payload shaping, focused workspace CLI tests, delegation docs
context_budget.recoverable later: older archived delegation and posture slices, broad product-compression history, unrelated planning-package internals
context_budget.externalize before shift: the minimum runtime-resolution vocabulary, the trust-classification rule, and the exact worker packet shape expected from the lane
context_budget.tiny resumability note: start from issue #286 compact answer, not from a broad delegation rewrite; preserve planner-only closure authority
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after the first runtime-resolution implementation milestone, after any config-shape decision, or before handing work to a weaker executor

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: make runtime posture selection and strong handoff cheap enough that ordinary tasks can be routed safely without planner reconstruction each time
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: portable planning truth stays portable; weaker executors do not own lane closure; lower-trust outcomes remain visible; local execution posture stays local
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: field names, exact compact payload shape, minimum runtime-resolution categories, and whether one or two focused tests best prove the answer
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the worker would need to redefine lane scope, add new planning authorities, or decide that lower-trust execution should count as full closeout

references.label: portable execplan semantics vs local execution posture

active_milestone.scope: Implement the compact runtime-resolution answer first, then use it to shape the compact strong-reasoning handoff packet and lower-trust delegation closeout guidance.
active_milestone.optional_deps: gh issue refresh before implementation if #285/#286/#289 wording changes materially

immediate_next_action: Translate issue #286 into a single compact runtime-resolution output contract, then mark one bounded implementation slice active against that contract.
invariants: Portable planning truth must remain portable.
Local posture truth must remain local.
Delegated implementation and lane closure are not the same authority.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: The runtime-resolution answer must be queryable from compact workspace surfaces before implementation is considered complete.
Manual external targets such as ChatGPT remain represented as explicit lower-trust execution methods unless proven otherwise.
Planner-authored handoff packets remain bounded artifacts, not open-ended worker journals.
open_questions_to_close: Which minimum posture categories are sufficient for the compact runtime-resolution answer: local, stronger-reasoning, manual external, or a slightly richer set?
Should the strong handoff packet live in defaults/config/report output or be a dedicated focused command projection?
What proof is sufficient to call a delegated result normal-trust rather than lower-trust?
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "delegation or runtime or summary or config"
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section startup --format json
completion_criteria: A compact runtime-resolution answer is available from ordinary workspace surfaces and combines planning semantics with local execution posture.
The answer clearly distinguishes stay-local, stronger-reasoning, and manual external paths without silent fallback.
A compact strong-reasoning handoff packet can be generated from the same bounded contract.
Focused tests prove lower-trust delegation remains visibly lower trust at closeout.

execution_run.what happened: Implemented _runtime_resolution_payload and _strong_handoff_packet_template helpers in workspace CLI; surfaced runtime_resolution and strong_handoff_packet in _mixed_agent_payload output and in _defaults_payload mixed_agent defaults block; added 8 focused tests covering defaults policy, config payload structure, and unit-level posture resolution scenarios.
execution_run.changed surfaces: cli.py (two new helpers + payload additions), test_workspace_cli.py (8 new tests)
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q (128 passed); new tests cover stay-local/stronger-reasoning/external-delegation/manual-handoff resolution paths and strong_handoff_packet template structure
execution_run.result for continuation: runtime_resolution and strong_handoff_packet are queryable from ordinary workspace surfaces; issues #286 and #289 done boundaries are satisfied; issue #285 parent intent requires verifying the full acceptance criteria before closing
execution_run.next step: run full validation suite, then close issues #285, #286, #289 and archive this plan

finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: moderate until the first worker packet is exercised

proof_report.validation proof: planned: focused workspace CLI tests plus defaults/config/report checks and planning-surface health check

intent_satisfaction.original intent: prepare a full execplan that lets a strong planner shape and bound implementation for the runtime-posture-selective-handoff lane
intent_satisfaction.was original intent fully satisfied?: yes for planning preparation, no for lane implementation
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: the lane now has a bounded machine-first execplan with explicit slice order, validation, stop conditions, and delegation rails

closure_check.why this decision is honest: the plan is prepared, but the lane's implementation and proof have not started
closure_check.evidence carried forward: this execplan plus lane references in state.toml
closure_check.reopen trigger: revisit if issue scope changes or the first runtime-resolution slice proves too wide for one bounded pass

execution_summary.outcome delivered: prepared a full machine-first execplan for the runtime posture lane

drift_log: 2026-04-24: Initial full-lane execplan created from current roadmap order and live issue set.
